1379	\begin{definition}
1380	[Rectangular matrix homomorphism of free sheaves]
1381	  \label{def:gr_matrixEndRect}
1382	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEndRect}
1383	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
1384	  Let \(S\) be a scheme, \(d, r \in \mathbb{N}\), and
1385	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d \times r}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\) a rectangular matrix
1386	  of global functions. The \emph{rectangular matrix homomorphism}
1387	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)\) is the morphism of free sheaves
1388	  \[
1389	    \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M) \;:\; \mathcal{O}_S^{\,r}
1390	      \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}
1391	  \]
1392	  whose \((k,\ell)\)-component -- \(k \in \{1,\dots,d\}\) an output index,
1393	  \(\ell \in \{1,\dots,r\}\) an input index -- under the presentation of
1394	  \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,r}\) and \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) as finite biproducts of copies of the
1395	  unit module \(\mathbf 1\), is the scalar endomorphism \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{k,\ell})\)
1396	  (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}). It is assembled from these components by the universal property
1397	  of the two biproducts, exactly as the square \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\)
1398	  (\cref{def:gr_matrixEnd}) and the chart quotient (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}). When
1399	  \(r = d\) it specialises to \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\); and the chart quotient \(u^I\)
1400	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) is by construction \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(X^I)\) of the
1401	  universal \(d \times r\) matrix \(X^I\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}).
1402	\end{definition}
1403	
1404	\begin{lemma}
1405	[Rectangular matrix homomorphism is natural under pullback]
1406	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback}
1407	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEndRect_pullback}
1408	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect, def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback,
1409	    lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, def:modules_pullbackComp, lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}
1410	  Let \(p : T \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and
1411	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(S,\mathcal{O}_S))\), with entrywise
1412	  base-changed matrix \(p^{\sharp}M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(T,\mathcal{O}_T))\).
1413	  Write \(Q_r : p^{*}\mathcal{O}_S^{\,r} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\) and
1414	  \(Q_d : p^{*}\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}\) for the
1415	  free-pullback comparisons (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}) at the index sets
1416	  \(\{1,\dots,r\}\) and \(\{1,\dots,d\}\). Then the pullback of
1417	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)\) is conjugate, through \(Q_r\) and \(Q_d\), to the
1418	  rectangular homomorphism of the base-changed matrix:
1419	  \[
1420	    p^{*}\bigl(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)\bigr)
1421	      \;=\; Q_d^{-1} \,\circ\, \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(p^{\sharp}M) \,\circ\, Q_r .
1422	  \]
1423	\end{lemma}
1424	\begin{proof}
1425	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect, lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
1426	    def:modules_pullbackComp, lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}
1427	  Identical to the square case \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}, with the source and target
1428	  biproducts now indexed by \(\{1,\dots,r\}\) and \(\{1,\dots,d\}\) respectively. The
1429	  pullback functor \(p^{*}\), a left adjoint, is additive and biproduct-preserving; through
1430	  the coproduct comparisons underlying the free-pullback isomorphisms
1431	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} it carries the biproduct presentation of
1432	  \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,r}\) (resp. \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\)) to that of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\)
1433	  (resp. \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}\)), the reassociation of the iterated pullbacks being
1434	  \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}. On each single \((k,\ell)\)-component the pulled-back
1435	  scalar endomorphism is, by \cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback}, the comparison-conjugate of
1436	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}((p^{\sharp}M)_{k,\ell})\). Reassembling over the two biproducts, the
1437	  conjugating one-element comparisons combine into \(Q_r\) on the source and \(Q_d\) on the
1438	  target, giving \(p^{*}(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)) = Q_d^{-1}\circ
1439	  \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(p^{\sharp}M)\circ Q_r\).
1440	\end{proof}
1441	
1442	\begin{lemma}
1443	[Square-after-rectangular composition law]
1444	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp}
1445	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEndRect_comp}
1446	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect, def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp, lem:gr_scalarEnd_add,
1447	    lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}
1448	  For \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(S,\mathcal{O}_S))\) and
1449	  \(N \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times d}(\Gamma(S,\mathcal{O}_S))\), post-composing the
1450	  rectangular homomorphism with a square one realises the matrix product, with the order
1451	  reversed by the contravariance of the component indexing exactly as in
1452	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}:

1452	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}:
1453	  \[
1454	    \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M) \,\circ\, \mathrm{matrixEnd}(N)
1455	      \;=\; \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(N \cdot M),
1456	  \]
1457	  the composite \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,r} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)}
1458	  \mathcal{O}_S^{\,d} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{matrixEnd}(N)} \mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) (here
1459	  \(N \cdot M\) is the \(d \times r\) product).
1460	\end{lemma}
1461	\begin{proof}
1462	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect, def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp, lem:gr_scalarEnd_add,
1463	    lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}
1464	  The same biproduct-extensionality computation as \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}, the only
1465	  change being that the leftmost (input) biproduct is indexed by \(\{1,\dots,r\}\) rather
1466	  than \(\{1,\dots,d\}\). The composite of two biproduct-assembled morphisms is again
1467	  biproduct-assembled, with \((k,\ell)\)-component the sum over the middle index \(m\) of the
1468	  composites of components, \(\sum_m \mathrm{scalarEnd}(N_{k,m}) \circ
1469	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{m,\ell})\). Each composite of scalar endomorphisms is the scalar
1470	  endomorphism of the product (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp}) and a finite sum of scalar
1471	  endomorphisms is the scalar endomorphism of the sum (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}); hence
1472	  that component is \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(\sum_m N_{k,m} M_{m,\ell}) =
1473	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}((N\cdot M)_{k,\ell})\), the \((k,\ell)\)-component of
1474	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(N\cdot M)\).
1475	\end{proof}
1476	
1477	\begin{lemma}
1478	[Adjunction transpose of the chart-overlap condition]
1479	  \label{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose}
1480	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose}
1481	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_bundleTransition, def:gr_modules_glueHom,
1482	    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app,
1483	    lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
1484	    def:modules_pullbackComp}
1485	  Fix size-\(d\) subsets \(I, J\) and let \(f_{IJ} : U^I_J \hookrightarrow U^I\),
1486	  \(t_{IJ} : U^I_J \xrightarrow{\sim} U^J_I\) be the overlap immersion and transition
1487	  (\cref{def:gr_transition}). The per-chart components \(u^I\) of the tautological quotient
1488	  are the adjoint transposes, along the chart immersions, of the chart quotients
1489	  \cref{def:gr_chart_quotient} (precomposed with the free-pullback comparison). Their
1490	  descent compatibility -- the hypothesis the gluing primitive \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom}
1491	  consumes, namely that the bundle transition \(g_{I,J}\) (\cref{def:gr_bundleTransition})
1492	  intertwines the pullbacks of \(u^I\) and \(u^J\) over \(U^I_J\) -- is equivalent, under the
1493	  pullback--pushforward adjunction of the overlap immersion, to the pullback-level identity
1494	  of morphisms \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,d}\)
1495	  \[
1496	    g_{I,J} \,\circ\, f_{IJ}^{*}\,u^I
1497	      \;=\; (t_{IJ} \circ f_{JI})^{*}\,u^J,
1498	  \]
1499	  understood through the free-pullback comparisons (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}) on
1500	  source and target.
1501	\end{lemma}
1502	\begin{proof}
1503	  \uses{lem:gr_homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app, lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib,
1504	    lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, def:modules_pullbackComp}
1505	  The components are produced by applying the adjunction hom-equivalence to the chart
1506	  quotients; the descent condition of \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom} is stated on those
1507	  transposes. Transposing back across the adjunction is the naturality of the conjugate
1508	  (mate) construction against the hom-equivalences, \cref{lem:gr_homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app},
1509	  together with the compatibility of the conjugate with the iterated-pullback comparison
1510	  \cref{lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib} reassociating the two pullbacks
1511	  \(f_{IJ}^{*}\) and \((t_{IJ}\circ f_{JI})^{*}\) via \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}. The
1512	  free-pullback comparisons \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} re-present both sides as morphisms
1513	  of the trivial bundles \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,d}\), under
1514	  which the transposed condition becomes the displayed pullback-level identity.
1515	\end{proof}

1517	\begin{lemma}
1518	[Overlap compatibility of the tautological quotient]
1519	  \label{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotient_overlap}
1520	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.tautologicalQuotient_overlap}
1521	  \uses{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback,
1522	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp, def:gr_bundleTransition, def:gr_chart_quotient,
1523	    def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap,
1524	    lem:gr_imageMatrix_map_eq, lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport,
1525	    lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_left}
1526	  For every pair of size-\(d\) subsets \(I, J\), the chart quotients \(u^I\)
1527	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) satisfy the overlap-compatibility condition required by the
1528	  section-level descent \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom} for the bundle gluing \((g_{I,J})\):
1529	  the pullback-level identity \(g_{I,J} \circ f_{IJ}^{*} u^I = (t_{IJ}\circ f_{JI})^{*} u^J\)
1530	  of \cref{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose} holds on each overlap \(U^I_J\).
1531	\end{lemma}
1532	\begin{proof}
1533	  \uses{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback,
1534	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp, def:gr_bundleTransition,
1535	    lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap, lem:gr_imageMatrix_map_eq,
1536	    lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_left}
1537	  By \cref{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose} it suffices to prove the
1538	  pullback-level identity \(g_{I,J} \circ f_{IJ}^{*} u^I = (t_{IJ}\circ f_{JI})^{*} u^J\) on
1539	  \(U^I_J\). Both sides are morphisms of trivial bundles \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,r} \to
1540	  \mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,d}\); write each as \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}\) of a \(d \times r\)
1541	  matrix over the overlap ring \(R^I_J\) and compare matrices.
1542	
1543	  Since \(u^I = \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(X^I)\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}), the
1544	  base-change naturality \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback} identifies \(f_{IJ}^{*} u^I\),
1545	  through the free-pullback comparisons, with \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}\) of the localised
1546	  matrix \(X^I\) over \(R^I_J\) (the global-sections base change is the away-localisation
1547	  \cref{lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_left}). The bundle transition \(g_{I,J}\) is
1548	  \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\) of the Cramer inverse \((X^I_J)^{-1}\)
1549	  (\cref{def:gr_bundleTransition}, \cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}); hence by the
1550	  square-after-rectangular composition law \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp},
1551	  \[
1552	    g_{I,J} \circ f_{IJ}^{*} u^I
1553	      \;=\; \mathrm{matrixEndRect}\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} \cdot X^I\bigr).
1554	  \]
1555	  The transition image matrix identity \(X^J = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\)
1556	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap}, transported across the overlap by
1557	  \cref{lem:gr_imageMatrix_map_eq}) turns the right-hand matrix into \(X^J\). Symmetrically,
1558	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}_J\) of \(X^J\) is, by \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback} along
1559	  \(t_{IJ}\circ f_{JI}\), the comparison form of \((t_{IJ}\circ f_{JI})^{*} u^J\). Therefore
1560	  the two sides agree, which is the required overlap compatibility.
1561	\end{proof}

1563	\begin{definition}
1564	
1565	\leanok
1566	[The tautological quotient \(u : \mathcal{O}^{r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\)]
1567	  \label{def:tautological_quotient}
1568	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.tautologicalQuotient}
1569	  \uses{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf, def:gr_chart_quotient,
1570	    def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_cocycle,
1571	    def:scheme_modules_glue, def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso,
1572	    def:gr_modules_glueHom, lem:gr_tautologicalQuotient_overlap}
1573	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
1574	  % sub-section "Universal quotient" (read from
1575	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L908-L910).
1576	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1577	  % "This is compatible with the homomorphisms $u^I$,
1578	  %  so we get a surjective homomorphism
1579	  %  $u : \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U$."
1580	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
1581	  The chart quotients \(u^I : \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\)
1582	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) are compatible with the bundle gluing
1583	  \((g_{I,J})\) of \(\mathcal{U}\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}): over
1584	  each overlap \(U^I_J\) one has the identity of \(d \times r\) matrices
1585	  \[
1586	    g_{I,J}\, X^I \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I \;=\; X^J \qquad \text{on } U^I_J,
1587	  \]
1588	  which is precisely the defining formula of the chart transition
1589	  \(\theta_{I,J}\) (\cref{def:gr_transition}, \cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}). At the
1590	  module level this overlap compatibility -- the equalising condition consumed by the
1591	  section-level descent \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom} -- is exactly
1592	  \cref{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotient_overlap}, whose proof routes the matrix identity above
1593	  through the rectangular pullback/composition API
1594	  (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback}, \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp}) and the
1595	  adjunction transpose \cref{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose}.
1596	  Hence the local maps \(u^I\), transported through the trivialisations of
1597	  \(\mathcal{U}\), agree on overlaps and glue to a single surjective
1598	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}\)-linear homomorphism
1599	  \[
1600	    u \;:\; \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\,r}
1601	      \;\twoheadrightarrow\; \mathcal{U},
1602	  \]
1603	  the \emph{tautological} (or \emph{universal}) rank-\(d\) quotient of the trivial
1604	  rank-\(r\) sheaf. It is surjective because each \(u^I\) is (split) surjective and
1605	  surjectivity is local on \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\), and \(\mathcal{U}\) is locally
1606	  free of rank \(d\) by \cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}. The pair
1607	  \(\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle\) is the family of rank-\(d\) quotients carried
1608	  by \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) itself.
1609	\end{definition}
1610	
1611	\section{The functor of points}
1612	\label{sec:grquot_functor}
1613	
1614	The Grassmannian functor sends a scheme \(T\) to the set of \emph{equivalence classes} of
1615	rank-\(d\) quotients of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\). The following block packages a single such
1616	quotient, the equivalence relation on them, and the pullback action through which the
1617	functor acts on morphisms; the two coherence blocks after it isolate the comparison
1618	identities that make that action functorial.
1619	
1620	\begin{definition}[Rank-\(d\) quotients and their pullback action]
1621	  \label{def:gr_rankQuotient}
1622	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient,
1623	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.Rel,
1624	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_refl,
1625	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_symm,
1626	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_trans,
1627	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqSetoid,
1628	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqPullback,
1629	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqPullback_rel}
1630	  \uses{lem:gr_pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
1631	        def:is_locally_free_of_rank}
1632	  A \emph{rank-\(d\) quotient of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\)} on a scheme \(T\) is a triple
1633	  \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\) consisting of a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-modules
1634	  \(\mathcal{F}\) that is locally free of rank \(d\) (\cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank})
1635	  together with an epimorphism \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) out
1636	  of the trivial rank-\(r\) bundle. Two such quotients \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\)
1637	  and \(y = \langle \mathcal{F}', q'\rangle\) are \emph{equivalent}, written \(x \sim y\),
1638	  when there is an isomorphism \(f : \mathcal{F} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{F}'\) with
1639	  \(q \circ f = q'\) (equivalently \(\ker q = \ker q'\)). This relation is reflexive
1640	  (\(f = \mathrm{id}\)), symmetric (\(f \mapsto f^{-1}\)), and transitive (composition of the
1641	  witnessing isomorphisms), hence an equivalence relation -- the setoid whose quotient is the
1642	  functor's value at \(T\).
1643	
1644	  For a morphism \(\psi : T' \to T\) the \emph{pullback action} sends
1645	  \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) to
1646	  \[
1647	    \psi^{*}x \;=\;
1648	      \bigl\langle\, \psi^{*}\mathcal{F},\;
1649	        \mathcal{O}_{T'}^{\,r} \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }
1650	        \psi^{*}\bigl(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\bigr) \xrightarrow{\ \psi^{*}q\ }
1651	        \psi^{*}\mathcal{F} \,\bigr\rangle,
1652	  \]
1653	  re-presenting the source as the trivial bundle via the free-pullback isomorphism
1654	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}. The result is again a rank-\(d\) quotient: the composite is
1655	  an epimorphism because pullback, a left adjoint, preserves epimorphisms and the
1656	  re-presentation is an isomorphism, and \(\psi^{*}\mathcal{F}\) is locally free of rank \(d\)
1657	  by \cref{lem:gr_pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank}. The pullback action respects the equivalence
1658	  relation -- given \(f\) witnessing \(x \sim y\), the pulled-back isomorphism \(\psi^{*}f\)
1659	  witnesses \(\psi^{*}x \sim \psi^{*}y\), by functoriality of pullback -- so it descends to a
1660	  map of equivalence classes.
1661	\end{definition}
1662	
1663	The two blocks below record the comparison identities, between the free-pullback isomorphism
1664	\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} and Mathlib's pseudofunctor structure on pullback, that make
1665	the descended pullback action of \cref{def:gr_rankQuotient} functorial.
1666	
1667	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1668	[Identity coherence of the free-pullback comparison]
1669	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}
1670	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}
1671	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1672	  For the identity morphism \(\mathrm{id}_T\), the canonical comparison morphism of unit
1673	  modules \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*}\mathbf{1} \to \mathbf{1}\) -- the map underlying the
1674	  free-pullback isomorphism \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} at a one-element index -- coincides
1675	  with the component at \(\mathbf{1}\) of the pullback-identity isomorphism
1676	  \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*} \cong \mathrm{id}\).
1677	\end{lemma}
1678	
1679	\begin{proof}
1680	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1681	  The comparison morphism is the mate of \(\mathrm{id}_{\mathbf 1}\) under the
1682	  pullback--pushforward adjunction: the image of the identity of \(\mathbf 1\) through the
1683	  adjunction's hom-equivalence on the unit. For the identity scheme morphism the pushforward
1684	  is the identity functor, so the adjunction unit is itself an identity; Mathlib's
1685	  pullback-identity isomorphism is exactly the conjugate (left-adjoint transpose) of the
1686	  pushforward-identity isomorphism. Tracing the identity through the hom-equivalence and the
1687	  conjugate-transpose identity for the pullback-identity isomorphism shows the comparison
1688	  equals the \(\mathbf 1\)-component of that isomorphism.
1689	\end{proof}
1690	
1691	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1692	[Identity coherence at the free sheaf]
1693	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_id}
1694	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_id}
1695	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}
1696	  For the identity morphism \(\mathrm{id}_T\) and an index set \(I\), the free-pullback
1697	  isomorphism \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*}(\bigoplus_I \mathbf 1) \cong \bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\) coincides
1698	  with the component at the free sheaf \(\bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\) of the pullback-identity
1699	  isomorphism \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*} \cong \mathrm{id}\).
1700	\end{lemma}
1701	
1702	\begin{proof}
1703	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}
1704	  Both sides are cocones on the coproduct \(\bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\); by coproduct
1705	  extensionality it suffices to check equality after precomposing with each coprojection
1706	  \(\mathbf 1 \to \bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\), which reduces to the one-element case
1707	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}.
1708	\end{proof}
1709	
1710	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1711	[Composite coherence of the free-pullback comparison]
1712	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
1713	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
1714	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id, def:modules_pullbackComp}
1715	  For composable scheme morphisms \(a : T'' \to T'\) and \(b : T' \to T\), the comparison
1716	  morphism of unit modules \((b \circ a)^{*}\mathbf{1} \to \mathbf{1}\) factors as
1717	  \(a^{*}\) of the comparison for \(b\), the comparison for \(a\), and the component at
1718	  \(\mathbf{1}\) of the iterated-pullback comparison \(a^{*}b^{*} \cong (b\circ a)^{*}\)
1719	  (\cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}).
1720	\end{lemma}
1721	
1722	\begin{proof}
1723	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id, def:modules_pullbackComp,
1724	    lem:gr_homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app}
1725	  This is the composite analogue of \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}: the mate
1726	  construction is compatible with composition of adjunctions, so the comparison for
1727	  \(b \circ a\) is the pasting of the comparisons for \(a\) and \(b\) reassociated by the
1728	  pseudofunctor comparison \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}. The compatibility of the mate
1729	  with the adjunction hom-equivalences is supplied by
1730	  \cref{lem:gr_homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app}, the reusable conjugate-naturality engine. The
1731	  pentagon coherence of the iterated-pullback comparisons makes the factorisation
1732	  well-defined.
1733	\end{proof}
1734	
1735	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1736	[Mate/conjugate compatibility of the adjunction hom-equivalence]
1737	  \label{lem:gr_homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app}
1738	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app}
1739	  Let \(\mathrm{adj}_1, \mathrm{adj}_2\) be two adjunctions and \(\alpha\) a natural
1740	  transformation between their left adjoints. For a morphism \(f\) the hom-equivalence of
1741	  \(\mathrm{adj}_2\) applied to \(\alpha_c \circ f\) equals the hom-equivalence of
1742	  \(\mathrm{adj}_1\) applied to \(f\), post-composed with the component at \(d\) of the
1743	  conjugate (mate) of \(\alpha\). This is the general naturality of the conjugate
1744	  construction against the two hom-equivalences; it is the reusable engine behind
1745	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}.
1746	\end{lemma}
1747	
1748	\begin{proof}
1749	  Both sides unfold to the same pasting of unit/counit triangles for the two adjunctions;
1750	  the equality is the standard compatibility of the mate of \(\alpha\) with the
1751	  hom-set bijections.
1752	\end{proof}
1753	
1754	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1755	[Composite coherence at the free sheaf]
1756	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_comp}
1757	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_comp}
1758	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp, def:modules_pullbackComp}
1759	  The composite analogue of \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_id}: for composable \(a, b\) and an
1760	  index set \(I\), the free-pullback isomorphism for \(b \circ a\) at \(\bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\)
1761	  factors through \(a^{*}\) of the free-pullback isomorphism for \(b\), the one for \(a\), and
1762	  the iterated-pullback comparison \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}.
1763	\end{lemma}
1764	
1765	\begin{proof}
1766	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
1767	  By coproduct extensionality it suffices to check the identity after precomposing with each
1768	  coprojection \(\mathbf 1 \to \bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\), which reduces to the one-element
1769	  composite coherence \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp} (with the base-ring sheaf
1770	  tracked through each pullback).
1771	\end{proof}
1772	
1773	Together \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id} and \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
1774	upgrade to the corresponding free-sheaf coherences -- the free-pullback isomorphism at the
1775	identity equals the component of the pullback-identity isomorphism, and likewise for the
1776	composite -- by coproduct extensionality over the decomposition
1777	\(\mathcal{O}^{\,\oplus I} = \bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\). These coherences discharge both
1778	functoriality laws (identity and composition) of the Grassmannian
1779	functor \cref{def:grassmannian_functor}.
1780	
1781	\begin{definition}
1782	
1783	\leanok
1784	[The Grassmannian / Quot-of-trivial-sheaf functor \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\)]
1785	  \label{def:grassmannian_functor}
1786	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor}
1787	  \uses{def:is_locally_free_of_rank, def:quot_functor, def:gr_rankQuotient,
1788	    lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id, lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
1789	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
1790	  % Exercise (2) "Grassmannian as a Quot scheme" (read from
1791	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L557-L564).
1792	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1793	  % "represents the
1794	  %  contravariant functor
1795	  %  $$\Grass(r,d) = \Quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}$$
1796	  %  from schemes to sets, which associates to any $T$ the
1797	  %  set of all equivalence classes
1798	  %  $\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients $q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F$
1799	  %  where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$."
1800	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2).}
1801	  The \emph{Grassmannian functor}
1802	  \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d) = \Quot^{\,d,\,\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}}_{\oplus^{r}
1803	  \mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}/\mathbb{Z}/\mathbb{Z}}\) is the contravariant functor
1804	  from schemes to sets defined as follows. For a scheme \(T\), a \emph{family of
1805	  rank-\(d\) quotients of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\)} is a pair
1806	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\) consisting of a locally free
1807	  \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\) of rank \(d\) together with a surjective
1808	  \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-linear homomorphism \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}
1809	  \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\). Two such families
1810	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\) and \(\langle \mathcal{F}', q' \rangle\) are
1811	  \emph{equivalent} if there is an isomorphism \(f : \mathcal{F}
1812	  \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{F}'\) with \(f \circ q = q'\); equivalently
1813	  \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\) as subsheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\). Set
1814	  \[
1815	    \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(T)
1816	      \;=\; \bigl\{\, \text{equivalence classes } \langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle
1817	        \text{ of rank-}d\text{ quotients of } \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \,\bigr\}.
1818	  \]
1819	  For a morphism \(\varphi : T' \to T\), the map
1820	  \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(\varphi)\) sends \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\)
1821	  to the pulled-back quotient \(\langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{F},\, \varphi^{*}q
1822	  \rangle\); this is well-defined on equivalence classes because pullback is right
1823	  exact and preserves local freeness of rank \(d\). (As \(\mathcal{F}\) is locally
1824	  free of rank \(d\), surjectivity is preserved and the support is automatically
1825	  proper -- all of \(T'\) -- so this is the special case
1826	  \(E = \mathcal{O}^{r}\), \(X = S = \operatorname{base}\) of the general Quot
1827	  functor of \cref{def:quot_functor}.)
1828	\end{definition}
1829	
1830	\section{The universal property}
1831	\label{sec:grquot_universal}
1832	
1833	\begin{theorem}
1834	
1835	\leanok
1836	[\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) represents the Grassmannian functor]
1837	  \label{thm:grassmannian_universal_property}
1838	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.represents}
1839	  \uses{def:grassmannian_functor, def:tautological_quotient,
1840	    def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf, def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_affine_chart,
1841	    def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_transition, lem:gr_cocycle}
1842	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
1843	  % Exercise (2) "Grassmannian as a Quot scheme" (read from
1844	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L546-L566).
1845	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1846	  % "Show that $\grass(r,d)$ together with the quotient
1847	  %  $u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U$ represents the
1848	  %  contravariant functor
1849	  %  $$\Grass(r,d) = \Quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}$$
1850	  %  from schemes to sets, which associates to any $T$ the
1851	  %  set of all equivalence classes
1852	  %  $\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients $q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F$
1853	  %  where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$.
1854	  %  Therefore, $\quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}$
1855	  %  exists, and equals $\grass(r,d)$."
1856	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2).}
1857	  The Grassmannian scheme \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}),
1858	  together with the tautological quotient \(\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle\)
1859	  (\cref{def:tautological_quotient}), represents the functor
1860	  \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:grassmannian_functor}). That is, for
1861	  every scheme \(T\) the natural map
1862	  \[
1863	    \operatorname{Hom}(T, \mathrm{Gr}(r,d))
1864	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
1865	    \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(T),
1866	    \qquad
1867	    \bigl(\,\varphi : T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\,\bigr)
1868	      \;\longmapsto\;
1869	    \langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U},\, \varphi^{*}u \rangle,
1870	  \]
1871	  is a bijection, natural in \(T\). Consequently the Quot scheme
1872	  \(\quot^{\,d,\,\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}}_{\oplus^{r}\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}/
1873	  \mathbb{Z}/\mathbb{Z}}\) of rank-\(d\) quotients of the trivial rank-\(r\) sheaf
1874	  exists and equals \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\); the tautological quotient is the
1875	  universal family.
1876	\end{theorem}
1877	
1878	\begin{proof}
1879	  \uses{def:tautological_quotient, def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf,
1880	    def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_universal_matrix,
1881	    def:gr_transition, lem:gr_cocycle, def:gr_glued_scheme,
1882	    def:grassmannian_functor}
1883	  Naturality of \(\varphi \mapsto \langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U}, \varphi^{*}u
1884	  \rangle\) is immediate: pullback is functorial and \(\varphi^{*}u\) is again a
1885	  surjection onto a rank-\(d\) locally free sheaf, so the assignment defines a
1886	  morphism of functors \(\operatorname{Hom}(-,\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)) \to
1887	  \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\). It remains to show it is a bijection for each
1888	  \(T\); we exhibit an inverse, constructed Zariski-locally on \(T\) and glued.
1889	
1890	  \emph{From a quotient to a morphism.} Let \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle \in
1891	  \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(T)\), so \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \twoheadrightarrow
1892	  \mathcal{F}\) with \(\mathcal{F}\) locally free of rank \(d\). For a size-\(d\)
1893	  subset \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) let \(T_I \subseteq T\) be the locus where
1894	  the composite
1895	  \[
1896	    \mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}
1897	      \overset{\ \iota_I\ }{\hookrightarrow} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}
1898	      \xrightarrow{\ q\ } \mathcal{F},
1899	  \]
1900	  with \(\iota_I\) the inclusion of the \(I\)-indexed coordinates, is an
1901	  isomorphism. This locus is open: in a local trivialisation of \(\mathcal{F}\)
1902	  the composite is a \(d \times d\) matrix and \(T_I\) is the non-vanishing locus
1903	  of its determinant. The \(T_I\) cover \(T\): at any point \(t\), the
1904	  \(d\)-dimensional fibre quotient \(q(t) : \kappa(t)^{r} \twoheadrightarrow
1905	  \mathcal{F}\otimes\kappa(t)\) is surjective, so some \(d\) of the \(r\) standard
1906	  basis vectors map to a basis of the \(d\)-dimensional fibre, i.e.\ \(t \in T_I\)
1907	  for that \(I\) (by Nakayama the isomorphism then holds in a neighbourhood).
1908	
1909	  On \(T_I\), trivialise \(\mathcal{F}\) using \(\iota_I^{*}\): the quotient
1910	  becomes a surjection \(q_I : \mathcal{O}_{T_I}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{T_I}^{\,d}\)
1911	  represented by a \(d \times r\) matrix \(M^I\) whose \(I\)-th minor \(M^I_I\) is
1912	  the identity \(1_{d \times d}\) -- exactly the shape of the universal matrix
1913	  \(X^I\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}). The \(d(r-d)\) free entries of \(M^I\)
1914	  are global functions on \(T_I\) and hence determine a unique
1915	  \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra homomorphism \(R^I = \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to
1916	  \Gamma(T_I, \mathcal{O}_{T_I})\) sending \(x^I_{p,q}\) to the corresponding entry
1917	  of \(M^I\), i.e.\ a unique morphism \(\varphi_I : T_I \to U^I\)
1918	  (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) with \(\varphi_I^{*}X^I = M^I\) and thus
1919	  \(\varphi_I^{*}u^I = q_I\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}).
1920	
1921	  \emph{Gluing.} On the overlap \(T_I \cap T_J\) the two trivialisations differ by
1922	  the change-of-basis \((M^I_J)^{-1}\), which is exactly the bundle transition
1923	  \(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}\) of \(\mathcal{U}\)
1924	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}); concretely
1925	  \(M^J = (M^I_J)^{-1} M^I = \varphi_I^{*}\theta_{I,J}(X^J)\)
1926	  (\cref{def:gr_transition}, \cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}). Hence
1927	  \(\varphi_I\) and \(\varphi_J\) land in the overlap charts and agree there after
1928	  the transition \(\theta_{I,J}\), so by the cocycle condition
1929	  (\cref{lem:gr_cocycle}) the \(\varphi_I\) glue to a single morphism
1930	  \(\varphi : T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}). By
1931	  construction \(\varphi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle = \langle \mathcal{F},
1932	  q \rangle\), since this holds on each \(T_I\) and the equivalence class is
1933	  determined by \(\ker(q)\).
1934	
1935	  \emph{Uniqueness.} If \(\psi : T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) also satisfies
1936	  \(\psi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle = \langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\), then
1937	  on \(\psi^{-1}(U^I)\) the pulled-back tautological quotient \(\psi^{*}u^I\)
1938	  equals \(q\) in the \(I\)-trivialisation, which forces \(\psi^{*}X^I = M^I\) and
1939	  hence \(\psi = \varphi_I\) there; as the \(U^I\) cover \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\),
1940	  \(\psi = \varphi\). Thus \(\varphi\) is the unique morphism inducing
1941	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\), and \(\varphi \mapsto \langle
1942	  \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U}, \varphi^{*}u \rangle\) is bijective.
1943	
1944	  Therefore \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) with \(\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle\) is a fine
1945	  moduli space: it represents \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\), so
1946	  \(\quot^{\,d,\,\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}}_{\oplus^{r}\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}/
1947	  \mathbb{Z}/\mathbb{Z}}\) exists and equals \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\).
1948	\end{proof}
1949

1	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GrassmannianCells
2	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotScheme
3	
4	/-!
5	# The tautological quotient and the universal property of `Gr(r,d)`
6	
7	This file adds, on top of the Grassmannian scheme `Gr(d,r)` built in
8	`GrassmannianCells.lean`, the tautological rank-`d` quotient
9	`u : O^r ↠ U` and the universal property making `Gr(d,r)` the fine moduli
10	space of rank-`d` locally free quotients of `O^r`.
11	
12	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianQuot.tex` (Nitsure §1).
13	-/
14	
15	universe u
16	
17	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite
18	
19	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
20	
21	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — scalar endomorphisms of the structure sheaf
22	
23	To realise a matrix of regular functions as a morphism of free sheaves of modules we
24	need to turn a global section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)` of the structure sheaf into a scalar
25	endomorphism of `O_X` (= `SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf`). On the affine chart
26	`U^I = Spec R^I` the matrix entries of the universal matrix `X^I` live in `R^I`, and we
27	inject them into the global sections via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`.
28	
29	These helpers are project-local: Mathlib has no ready-made "matrix ↦ morphism of free
30	sheaves" primitive. -/
31	
32	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
33	
34	/-- The global section of the structure sheaf `O_X` (as a sheaf of modules over itself)
35	determined by a section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)` over the whole space, by restriction to every open.
36	Project-local helper for building scalar endomorphisms. -/
37	noncomputable def globalUnitSection (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
38	    (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).sections :=
39	  PresheafOfModules.sectionsMk
40	    (fun Y => (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y))
41	    (by
42	      intro Y Z f
43	      change (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f) (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a)
44	        = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a
45	      rw [← CategoryTheory.comp_apply, ← X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map_comp]
46	      congr 1)
47	
48	/-- The scalar endomorphism of `O_X` given by a global section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)`:
49	multiplication by `a`. Project-local helper. -/
50	noncomputable def scalarEnd (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
51	    SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
52	  (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv.symm (globalUnitSection a)
53	
54	/-- `scalarEnd 1` is the identity endomorphism of `O_X`. Project-local helper for
55	identifying the diagonal entries of the chart quotient. -/
56	lemma scalarEnd_one : scalarEnd (1 : Γ(X, ⊤)) = 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) := by
57	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
58	  ext Y
59	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (1 : Γ(X, ⊤))
60	      = (SheafOfModules.Hom.val (𝟙 (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))).app Y
61	          (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
62	  rw [SheafOfModules.id_val, PresheafOfModules.id_app]
63	  exact map_one _
64	
65	/-- `scalarEnd 0` is the zero endomorphism of `O_X`. Project-local helper for identifying
66	the off-diagonal entries of the chart quotient. -/
67	lemma scalarEnd_zero : scalarEnd (0 : Γ(X, ⊤)) = 0 := by
68	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
69	  ext Y
70	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (0 : Γ(X, ⊤))
71	      = (SheafOfModules.Hom.val
72	          (0 : SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).app Y
73	          (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
74	  refine (map_zero _).trans ?_
75	  rfl
76	
77	/-- The value of the scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` on a section `x` over `Y` is
78	multiplication by the restriction of `a`: `(scalarEnd a)(x) = x · a|_Y`. Project-local
79	helper, the computational heart of the `scalarEnd` ring-hom identities below. -/
80	lemma scalarEnd_val_app (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) (Y : (TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : TopCat))ᵒᵖ)
81	    (x : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y) :
82	    (scalarEnd a).val.app Y x = x * X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := by
83	  rfl
84	
85	/-- `scalarEnd c` corresponds to the global section `c` under `unitHomEquiv`. -/
86	lemma unitHomEquiv_scalarEnd (c : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
87	    (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv (scalarEnd c) = globalUnitSection c := by
88	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
89	
90	/-- The scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` sends the unit section `1` to `a|_Y`. -/
91	lemma scalarEnd_val_app_one (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) (Y : (TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : TopCat))ᵒᵖ) :
92	    (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
93	      = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := by
94	  exact one_smul _ _
95	
96	/-- Composition of scalar endomorphisms is multiplication: `scalarEnd a ≫ scalarEnd b =
97	scalarEnd (a * b)`. Project-local; underlies `matrixEnd` multiplicativity. -/
98	lemma scalarEnd_comp (a b : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
99	    scalarEnd a ≫ scalarEnd b = scalarEnd (a * b) := by
100	  apply (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv.injective
101	  change SheafOfModules.sectionsMap (scalarEnd b)
102	        ((SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv (scalarEnd a))
103	      = (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv (scalarEnd (a * b))
104	  rw [unitHomEquiv_scalarEnd, unitHomEquiv_scalarEnd]
105	  refine PresheafOfModules.sections_ext _ _ (fun Y => ?_)
106	  change (scalarEnd b).val.app Y (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a)
107	      = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (a * b)
108	  exact (RingHom.map_mul (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom a b).symm

113	    scalarEnd (a + b) = scalarEnd a + scalarEnd b := by
114	  conv_lhs => rw [scalarEnd]
115	  rw [Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
116	  refine PresheafOfModules.sections_ext _ _ (fun Y => ?_)
117	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (a + b)
118	      = (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
119	        + (scalarEnd b).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
120	  rw [scalarEnd_val_app_one, scalarEnd_val_app_one]
121	  exact RingHom.map_add (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom a b

126	  classical

128	  | empty => simp [scalarEnd_zero]
129	  | insert a s ha ih => rw [Finset.sum_insert ha, Finset.sum_insert ha, scalarEnd_add, ih]

131	/-! ## Matrix automorphisms of the free sheaf
132	
133	To realise the `GL_d` bundle transitions we promote an (invertible) `d × d` matrix of
134	global sections to an automorphism of the free rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`, exactly as
135	`chartQuotientMap` realises the universal matrix. The two key algebraic facts — that
136	`matrixEnd` turns matrix multiplication into composition and the identity matrix into the
137	identity — follow from the `scalarEnd` ring-hom identities above. -/
138	
139	/-- `SheafOfModules` over `O_S` has finite biproducts (it has finite products). -/
140	instance hasFiniteBiproducts_modules (S : Scheme.{0}) :
141	    HasFiniteBiproducts (SheafOfModules S.ringCatSheaf) :=
142	  HasFiniteBiproducts.of_hasFiniteProducts
143	
144	/-- A `d × d` matrix of global sections of `O_S` realised as an endomorphism of the free
145	rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`: the `(p,q)`-entry acts as `scalarEnd`, assembled over the rank-`d`
146	biproduct (mirrors `chartQuotientMap`). Project-local. -/
147	noncomputable def matrixEnd {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
148	    SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ⟶
149	      SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) :=
150	  (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).symm.hom ≫
151	    biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i)) ≫
152	    (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).hom
153	
154	/-- Composition of two `biproduct.matrix` morphisms is the matrix of pointwise sums of
155	composites — the categorical matrix product. Project-local helper for `matrixEnd_comp`. -/
156	private lemma biproduct_matrix_comp {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
157	    (mM mN : Fin d → Fin d →
158	      (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)) :
159	    biproduct.matrix (f := fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)
160	        (g := fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) mM ≫ biproduct.matrix mN
161	      = biproduct.matrix (fun i q => ∑ p, mM i p ≫ mN p q) := by
162	  refine biproduct.hom_ext' _ _ (fun i => biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun q => ?_))
163	  simp only [Category.assoc, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.matrix_π, biproduct.lift_desc,
164	    biproduct.ι_matrix, biproduct.lift_π]
165	
166	/-- `matrixEnd` turns matrix multiplication into composition (with the order reversed by
167	the contravariance of the column/component indexing): `matrixEnd M ≫ matrixEnd N =
168	matrixEnd (N * M)`. Project-local. -/
169	lemma matrixEnd_comp {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
170	    matrixEnd M ≫ matrixEnd N = matrixEnd (N * M) := by
171	  rw [matrixEnd, matrixEnd, matrixEnd]
172	  have hcomp : biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i))
173	        ≫ biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (N p i))
174	      = biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd ((N * M) p i)) := by
175	    rw [biproduct_matrix_comp]
176	    congr 1
177	    funext i q
178	    simp_rw [scalarEnd_comp]
179	    rw [← scalarEnd_sum, Matrix.mul_apply]
180	    exact congrArg scalarEnd (Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun p _ => mul_comm _ _))
181	  simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
182	  rw [← Category.assoc (biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i))), hcomp]

184	/-- `matrixEnd` of the identity matrix is the identity. Project-local. -/
185	lemma matrixEnd_one {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} :
186	    matrixEnd (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) = 𝟙 _ := by
187	  rw [matrixEnd]
188	  have hmat : biproduct.matrix
189	        (fun i p => scalarEnd ((1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) p i))
190	      = 𝟙 (⨁ fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) := by
191	    refine biproduct.hom_ext' _ _ (fun i => biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun p => ?_))
192	    simp only [Category.assoc, Category.id_comp, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.lift_π]
193	    rw [Matrix.one_apply]
194	    by_cases h : p = i
195	    · subst h; rw [if_pos rfl, scalarEnd_one, biproduct.ι_π_self]
196	    · rw [if_neg h, scalarEnd_zero, biproduct.ι_π_ne _ (Ne.symm h)]
197	  rw [hmat, Category.id_comp, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.inv_hom_id]

201	noncomputable def matrixToFreeIso {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
202	    (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) (hMN : M * N = 1) (hNM : N * M = 1) :
203	    SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ≅
204	      SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) where
205	  hom := matrixEnd M
206	  inv := matrixEnd N
207	  hom_inv_id := by rw [matrixEnd_comp, hNM, matrixEnd_one]
208	  inv_hom_id := by rw [matrixEnd_comp, hMN, matrixEnd_one]

211	    (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) (hMN : M * N = 1) (hNM : N * M = 1) :
212	    (matrixToFreeIso M N hMN hNM).hom = matrixEnd M := rfl

220	    (A A' B B' : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤))

224	  rw [matrixToFreeIso_hom, matrixToFreeIso_hom, matrixEnd_comp]

228	    (matrixToFreeIso M N hMN hNM).inv = matrixEnd N := rfl

573	/-! ### Pullback of free sheaves along an arbitrary scheme morphism
574	
575	The functor of points (`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor`) acts on morphisms by
576	pullback, and for that one needs `f^* (O^n) ≅ O^n` for an *arbitrary* scheme morphism
577	`f`. Mathlib's `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` supplies this only when the underlying
578	site functor `Opens.map f.base` is `Final`; the previous chapters discharged that `Final`
579	hypothesis only for open immersions and isomorphisms. The first lemma below removes the
580	restriction entirely: `Opens.map f.base` is `Final` for *every* scheme morphism, because
581	the structured-arrow category over any open `V` has a terminal object (the whole space
582	`⊤`). With that in hand, `pullbackFreeIso` and `pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` hold for all
583	morphisms. -/
584	
585	/-- For an arbitrary scheme morphism `φ`, the site functor `Opens.map φ.base` is `Final`:
586	over any open `V` of the target the structured-arrow category `{U : V ≤ φ⁻¹ U}` has the
587	terminal object `U = ⊤`, hence is connected. This is the missing ingredient that makes
588	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit`/`pullbackObjFreeIso` applicable to *every* morphism,
589	not just to open immersions. Project-local. -/
590	lemma opensMap_final {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) :
591	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base).Final := by
592	  constructor
593	  intro V
594	  set top : StructuredArrow V (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base) :=
595	    StructuredArrow.mk (Y := (⊤ : T.Opens)) (homOfLE le_top)
596	  haveI : Nonempty (StructuredArrow V (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base)) := ⟨top⟩
597	  apply zigzag_isConnected
598	  intro s t
599	  have hs : s ⟶ top := StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_top) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
600	  have ht : t ⟶ top := StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_top) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
601	  exact Relation.ReflTransGen.trans
602	    (Relation.ReflTransGen.single (Or.inl ⟨hs⟩))
603	    (Relation.ReflTransGen.single (Or.inr ⟨ht⟩))
604	
605	/-- **Pullback of a free sheaf of modules is free, for any scheme morphism**: for
606	`φ : T' ⟶ T` and an index type `I`, `φ^*(O_T^{⊕I}) ≅ O_{T'}^{⊕I}`. Built from
607	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` once `opensMap_final` supplies the `Final` instance.
608	Project-local. -/
609	noncomputable def pullbackFreeIso {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) (I : Type u) :
610	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)
611	      ≅ SheafOfModules.free (R := T'.ringCatSheaf) I := by
612	  haveI := opensMap_final φ
613	  exact SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso φ.toRingCatSheafHom I

618	lemma pullbackFreeIso_eqToHom {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ) (I : Type u) :
619	    eqToHom (congrArg
620	        (fun α => (Scheme.Modules.pullback α).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)) h)
621	        ≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).hom
622	      = (pullbackFreeIso φ I).hom := by
623	  subst h; simp
624	
625	/-- Iso-level free-pullback cancellation: for equal base morphisms `φ = ψ`, the composite
626	`pullbackFreeIso φ ≪≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ).symm` is the `eqToIso` transport between the
627	(differing) pullback sources. Proved generically (`φ`, `ψ` variables, `subst`), so applying
628	it never forces the kernel to whnf a concrete immersion — the leaner replacement for the
629	`.hom`-level cast chain in `bundleTransition_self`. Project-local. -/
630	lemma pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ)
631	    (I : Type u) :
632	    pullbackFreeIso φ I ≪≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).symm
633	      = eqToIso (congrArg
634	          (fun α => (Scheme.Modules.pullback α).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I))
635	          h) := by

638	/-- **Pullback preserves rank-`d` local freeness.** If `M` is locally free of rank `d` on
639	`T`, then `φ^* M` is locally free of rank `d` on `T'`, for any scheme morphism `φ`. The
640	chart cover `{U i}` of `T` trivialising `M` pulls back to the cover `{φ⁻¹ U i}` of `T'`;
641	on each member the restriction of `φ^* M` is identified with the pulled-back chart-free
642	sheaf via the pseudofunctor comparison `pullbackComp`, the factorisation
643	`φ ∘ (φ⁻¹ U i).ι = (φ ∣_ U i) ≫ (U i).ι` (`morphismRestrict_ι`), and `pullbackFreeIso`.

645	lemma pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) {M : T.Modules}
646	    {d : ℕ} (h : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank M d) :
647	    SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj M) d := by
648	  obtain ⟨ι, U, hcover, hloc⟩ := h
649	  refine ⟨ι, fun i => φ ⁻¹ᵁ (U i), Scheme.Hom.iSup_preimage_eq_top φ hcover, ?_⟩
650	  intro i
651	  obtain ⟨e⟩ := hloc i
652	  exact ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (φ ⁻¹ᵁ (U i)).ι φ).app M ≪≫
653	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (morphismRestrict_ι φ (U i)).symm).app M ≪≫
654	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (φ ∣_ (U i)) (U i).ι).app M).symm ≪≫
655	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (φ ∣_ (U i))).mapIso e ≪≫
656	    pullbackFreeIso (φ ∣_ (U i)) (ULift.{u} (Fin d))⟩

662	/-! ## The GL_d bundle transition cocycle
663	
664	The universal quotient `U` is glued from the per-chart free rank-`d` sheaves `O_{U^I}^d`
665	along the bundle transitions `g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)⁻¹`, realised as matrix automorphisms via
666	`matrixToFreeIso` and conjugated to the overlap pullbacks by `pullbackFreeIso`. This section
667	constructs `bundleTransition` and proves its self-identity (C1); the triple-overlap
668	multiplicativity (C2) is the matrix cocycle of `lem:gr_cocycle` transported to the common
669	overlap by `pullbackBaseChangeTransport`/`glueData_bridge_*`. -/
670	
671	/-- The Cramer inverse of the self-minor `X^I_I` is the identity: since `X^I_I = 1`
672	(`universalMatrix_submatrix_self`) its inverse is `1`. Project-local; underlies C1. -/
673	lemma universalMinorInv_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
674	    universalMinorInv d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
675	  have hmin : universalMinor d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
676	    rw [universalMinor, universalMatrix_submatrix_self, Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _)]
677	  rw [universalMinorInv, hmin, inv_one]
678	
679	/-- The injected Cramer inverse and minor matrices over the overlap structure sheaf are
680	mutually inverse — the `GL_d` invertibility hypotheses for `matrixToFreeIso`. Project-local. -/
681	private lemma bundleMatrix_cancel (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
682	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
683	    ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
684	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
685	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ)) *
686	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
687	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
688	        (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)) = 1 ∧
689	    ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
690	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
691	        (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)) *
692	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
693	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
694	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ)) = 1 := by
695	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
696	  · rw [← map_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1, map_one]
697	  · rw [← map_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).2, map_one]
698	
699	/-- The **bundle transition** `g_{I,J}` (`def:gr_bundleTransition`): the isomorphism of
700	sheaves of modules on the overlap `U^I_J` induced by the invertible matrix
701	`(X^I_J)⁻¹ = universalMinorInv d r I J`. It identifies the pullback of `O_{U^I}^d` along
702	`f_{IJ}` with the pullback of `O_{U^J}^d` along `t_{IJ} ≫ f_{JI}`, by conjugating the
703	matrix automorphism `matrixToFreeIso (X^I_J)⁻¹` (built like `chartQuotientMap`) by the
704	free-pullback comparisons `pullbackFreeIso`. -/
705	noncomputable def bundleTransition (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r))
706	    (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
707	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ)).obj
708	        (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) ≅
709	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartTransition d r I J hI hJ ≫ chartIncl d r J I hJ hI)).obj
710	        (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r J).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) :=
711	  Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (Fin d) ≪≫
712	    matrixToFreeIso
713	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
714	          (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
715	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ))
716	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
717	          (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
718	        (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ))
719	      (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1
720	      (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I J hI hJ).2 ≪≫
721	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso
722	      (chartTransition d r I J hI hJ ≫ chartIncl d r J I hJ hI) (Fin d)).symm
723	
724	/-- **Self-identity of the bundle transition (C1)** (`lem:gr_bundleCocycle_id`): on the
725	diagonal overlap `U^I_I` (where `t_{II} = 𝟙`) the bundle transition is the identity, in the
726	form required by the gluing primitive `Scheme.Modules.glue`. The matrix part is the identity
727	since `(X^I_I)⁻¹ = 1` (`universalMinorInv_self`), so `matrixEnd 1 = 𝟙` (`matrixEnd_one`); the
728	two free-pullback comparisons then cancel into the `eqToIso` transport.

1542	/-! ### Cast-collapse of `pullbackCongr` against the free-pullback comparisons
1543	
1544	The three transports of the bundle cocycle are interleaved with `pullbackCongr` casts
1545	(the `glueData_bridge_*` endpoint alignments). The next three lemmas collapse those casts
1546	against the free-pullback comparisons `pullbackFreeIso`. Each is generic in the (equal)
1547	base morphisms and proved by `subst`, so applying them never forces the kernel to whnf a
1548	concrete immersion (the `pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso` discipline). -/
1549	
1550	/-- Closed zig-zag: `Q_φ⁻¹ ≫ pullbackCongr(h).app ≫ Q_ψ = 𝟙` for equal base morphisms
1551	`φ = ψ`. Project-local helper for the C2 endpoint alignment. -/

1556	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr h).app
1557	          (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)).hom ≫
1558	        (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).hom
1559	      = 𝟙 _ := by
1560	  subst h
1561	  simp [Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr]
1562	
1563	/-- Left absorption: `pullbackCongr(h).app ≫ Q_ψ = Q_φ` for equal base morphisms `φ = ψ`.
1564	Project-local helper for the C2 endpoint alignment (source bridge). -/

1570	        (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).hom

1573	  simp [Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr]

1617	/-! ### Adjunction transposition of the descent-equalizer legs
1618	
1619	The overlap condition consumed by `glueLift` is an equation between composites of adjoint
1620	transposes along the chart immersions. The next two lemmas transpose each leg back across
1621	the pullback–pushforward adjunction of the *composite* overlap immersion, exposing the
1622	pullback-level identity `g_{ij} ∘ f_ij^* u^i = (t_ij ≫ f_ji)^* u^j` that the matrix

1631	`conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv`. Project-local. -/
1632	lemma homEquiv_comp_unit_pushforwardComp {V U X : Scheme.{u}} (b : V ⟶ U) (a : U ⟶ X)
1633	    {W : X.Modules} {M : U.Modules} (c : (Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj W ⟶ M) :
1634	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).homEquiv W M c ≫
1635	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward a).map
1636	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app M) ≫
1637	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp b a).hom.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj M))
1638	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (b ≫ a)).homEquiv W
1639	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj M)
1640	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).inv.app W ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map c) := by
1641	  -- inner transpose: `c ≫ unit_b` is the `b`-transpose of `(pullback b).map c`
1642	  have h2 : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).homEquiv
1643	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj W) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj M)
1644	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map c)
1645	      = c ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.app M := by
1646	    rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]
1647	    exact ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).unit.naturality c).symm
1648	  -- composite-adjunction transpose factors through the two single transposes
1649	  have h3 : ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).comp
1650	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b)).homEquiv W
1651	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj M) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map c)
1652	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).homEquiv _ _
1653	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b).homEquiv _ _
1654	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback b).map c)) := by
1655	    rw [Adjunction.comp_homEquiv]
1656	    rfl
1657	  -- mate compatibility: precomposing with `pullbackComp.inv` is postcomposition by the
1658	  -- conjugate, which Mathlib identifies as `pushforwardComp.hom`
1659	  have h4 := homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app
1660	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).comp
1661	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction b))
1662	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (b ≫ a))
1663	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp b a).inv

1670	    (congrArg (· ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp b a).hom.app

1818	/-! ### Restriction of the glued sheaf to a chart (`def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso`)

1820	The glued sheaf is the descent equalizer `eq(a, b) ⊆ ∏ᵢ (ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ`. This section
1821	re-exposes the two legs of that equalizer as standalone declarations
1822	(`glueLegA`/`glueLegB`), records that `glue` *is* their equalizer (`glueIsoEqualizer`,
1823	definitional), and produces the canonical projection `glueProj i` of the glued sheaf
1824	onto the `i`-th pushforward factor together with its compatibility with `glueLift`.
1825	The adjoint transpose of `glueProj i` along the chart immersion `D.ι i` is the
1826	*restriction morphism* `glueRestrictionHom i : ιᵢ^* (glue …) ⟶ M i`; effective descent
1827	(consuming the cocycle hypotheses C1/C2) makes it an isomorphism — the restriction
1828	isomorphism `glueRestrictionIso` of `def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso`. The
1829	limit-preservation engine is `restrictFunctor`: pullback along an open immersion is
1830	naturally isomorphic to a site-level pushforward, which is a right adjoint, hence
1831	preserves the descent equalizer and the pushforward product. -/

1833	/-- `restrictFunctor f` along an open immersion is a right adjoint: it is a site-level

1835	by the presheaf-pullback + sheafification construction. Project-local instance. -/
1836	instance restrictFunctor_isRightAdjoint {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] :

1841	  exact (SheafOfModules.PullbackConstruction.adjunction _).isRightAdjoint

3055	/-! ## The functor of points and the universal property
3056	
3057	The Grassmannian functor sends a scheme `T` to the set of *equivalence classes* of
3058	rank-`d` quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F` with `F` locally free of rank `d`. We encode a single
3059	such quotient as the structure `RankQuotient r d T`, the equivalence as `RankQuotient.Rel`
3060	(an isomorphism of the targets commuting with the quotient maps), and the functor's value
3061	at `T` as the quotient `Quotient (rqSetoid r d T)`. The pullback action `rqPullback`
3062	together with `pullbackFreeIso`/`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` (and the fact that the
3063	pullback functor preserves epimorphisms, being a left adjoint) realise functoriality.
3064	
3065	Because a sheaf of modules `F : T.Modules` is a large object, this quotient lives in
3066	`Type 1` (not `Type 0` as the original scaffold signature stated); the corrected universe
3067	is the only change to the pinned signature, and it is forced — the substantive content is
3068	unchanged. -/
